member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ApplicationBuilderExtensions
member.summary: Extension class on IApplicationBuilder to initialize the service provider of the TokenAcquirerFactory in ASP.NET Core.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ApplicationBuilderExtensions.UseTokenAcquirerFactory(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Builder.IApplicationBuilder)
member.summary: Uses the token acquirer factory, therefore

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpContextExtensions.StoreTokenUsedToCallWebAPI(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext,Microsoft.IdentityModel.Tokens.SecurityToken)
member.summary: Keep the validated token associated with the HTTP request.
member.param: HTTP context.
Token to preserve after the token is validated so that it can be used in the actions.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpContextExtensions.GetTokenUsedToCallWebAPI(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpContext)
member.summary: Get the parsed information about the token used to call the web API.
member.param: HTTP context associated with the current request.
member.returns: used to call the web API.

member.summary: Constructor of the TokenAcquisition service. This requires the Azure AD Options to configure the confidential client application and a token cache provider. This constructor is called by ASP.NET Core dependency injection.
member.param: The App token cache provider.
Host of the token acquisition service.
HTTP client factory.
Logger.
Service provider.
Credential loader service.

members.member:
Used in web APIs (no user interaction). Replies to the client through the HTTP response by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating the 'WWW-Authenticate' header so that the client, in turn, can trigger a user interaction so that the user consents to more scopes.
Scopes to consent to. The that triggered the challenge. The to update. if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionAspNetCore.ReplyForbiddenWithWwwAuthenticateHeader(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalUiRequiredException,System.String,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpResponse)
member.summary: Used in web APIs (no user interaction). Replies to the client through the HTTP response by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating the 'WWW-Authenticate' header so that the client, in turn, can trigger a user interaction so that the user consents to more scopes.
member.param: Scopes to consent to.
The that triggered the challenge.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
The to update.

members.member:
Used in web APIs (no user interaction). Replies to the client through the HTTP response by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating the 'WWW-Authenticate' header so that the client, in turn, can trigger a user interaction so that the user consents to more scopes.
Scopes to consent to. The that triggered the challenge. Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API. The to update. if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionAspNetCore.AddAccountToCacheFromAuthorizationCodeAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.OpenIdConnect.AuthorizationCodeReceivedContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: This handler is executed after the authorization code is received (once the user signs-in and consents) during the authorization code flow in a web app. It uses the code to request an access token from the Microsoft identity platform and caches the tokens and an entry about the signed-in user's account in the MSAL's token cache. The access token (and refresh token) provided in the , once added to the cache, are then used to acquire more tokens using the on-behalf-of flow for the signed-in user's account, in order to call to downstream APIs.
member.param: The context used when an 'AuthorizationCode' is received over the OpenIdConnect protocol.
scopes to request access to.
Authentication scheme to use (by default, OpenIdConnectDefaults.AuthenticationScheme).

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionAspnetCoreHost
member.summary: Implementation of ITokenAcquisitionHost in the case of ASP.NET Core

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionAspnetCoreHost.#ctor(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.IHttpContextAccessor,Microsoft.Identity.Web.IMergedOptionsStore,System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Constructor of the TokenAcquisition service. This requires the Azure AD Options to configure the confidential client application and a token cache provider. This constructor is called by ASP.NET Core dependency injection.
member.param: Access to the HttpContext of the request.
Configuration options.
Service provider.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.s_base64Table
member.summary: Encoding table.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.Encode(System.String)
member.summary: The following functions perform base64url encoding which differs from regular base64 encoding as follows * padding is skipped so the pad character '=' doesn't have to be percent encoded * the 62nd and 63rd regular base64 encoding characters ('+' and '/') are replace with ('-' and '_') The changes make the encoding alphabet file and URL safe.
member.param: string to encode.
member.returns: Base64Url encoding of the UTF8 bytes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.Encode(System.Byte[],System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Converts a subset of an array of 8-bit unsigned integers to its equivalent string representation that is encoded with base-64-url digits. Parameters specify the subset as an offset in the input array, and the number of elements in the array to convert.
member.param: An array of 8-bit unsigned integers.
The number of elements of inArray to convert.
An offset in inArray.
member.returns: The string representation in base 64 url encoding of length elements of inArray, starting at position offset.
member.exception: offset or length is negative OR offset plus length is greater than the length of inArray.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.Encode(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Converts a subset of an array of 8-bit unsigned integers to its equivalent string representation that is encoded with base-64-url digits. Parameters specify the subset as an offset in the input array, and the number of elements in the array to convert.
member.param: An array of 8-bit unsigned integers.
member.returns: The string representation in base 64 url encoding of length elements of inArray, starting at position offset.
member.exception: offset or length is negative OR offset plus length is greater than the length of inArray.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.DecodeBytes(System.String)
member.summary: Converts the specified string, which encodes binary data as base-64-url digits, to an equivalent 8-bit unsigned integer array.
member.param: base64Url encoded string.
member.returns: UTF8 bytes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Util.Base64UrlHelpers.Decode(System.String)
member.summary: Decodes the string from Base64UrlEncoded to UTF8.
member.param: string to decode.
member.returns: UTF8 string.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Extensibility.BaseAuthorizationHeaderProvider
member.summary: Base class for custom implementations of that would still want to leverage the default implementation for the bearer and Pop protocols.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Extensibility.BaseAuthorizationHeaderProvider.#ctor(System.IServiceProvider)

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants
member.summary: Constants for claim types.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Name
member.summary: Name claim: "name".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.ObjectId
member.summary: Old Object Id claim: http://schemas.microsoft.com/identity/claims/objectidentifier.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Oid
member.summary: New Object id claim: "oid".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.PreferredUserName
member.summary: PreferredUserName: "preferred_username".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.TenantId
member.summary: Old TenantId claim: "http://schemas.microsoft.com/identity/claims/tenantid".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Tid
member.summary: New Tenant Id claim: "tid".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.ClientInfo
member.summary: ClientInfo claim: "client_info".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.UniqueObjectIdentifier
member.summary: UniqueObjectIdentifier: "uid". Home Object Id.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.UniqueTenantIdentifier
member.summary: UniqueTenantIdentifier: "utid". Home Tenant Id.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Scope
member.summary: Older scope claim: "http://schemas.microsoft.com/identity/claims/scope".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Scp
member.summary: Newer scope claim: "scp".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Roles
member.summary: New Roles claim = "roles".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Role
member.summary: Old Role claim: "http://schemas.microsoft.com/ws/2008/06/identity/claims/role".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Sub
member.summary: Subject claim: "sub".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Acr
member.summary: Acr claim: "acr".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.UserFlow
member.summary: UserFlow claim: "http://schemas.microsoft.com/claims/authnclassreference".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Tfp
member.summary: Tfp claim: "tfp".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.NameIdentifierId
member.summary: Name Identifier ID claim: "http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/ws/2005/05/identity/claims/nameidentifier".

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Username
member.summary: Username claims for ROPC flow.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ClaimConstants.Password
member.summary: Password claims for ROPC flow.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants
member.summary: General constants for Microsoft Identity Web.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.LoginHint
member.summary: LoginHint. Represents the preferred_username claim in the ID token.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.DomainHint
member.summary: DomainHint. Determined by the tenant Id.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.Claims
member.summary: Claims. Determined from the signed-in user.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.Bearer
member.summary: Bearer. Predominant type of access token used with OAuth 2.0.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.AzureAd
member.summary: AzureAd. Configuration section name for AzureAd.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.AzureAdB2C
member.summary: AzureAdB2C. Configuration section name for AzureAdB2C.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.Scope
member.summary: Scope.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.Policy
member.summary: Policy for B2C user flows. The name of the policy to check against a specific user flow.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.SpaAuthCode
member.summary: SpaAuthCode. Key to retreive SpaAuthCode from the HttpContext.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.ResetPasswordPath
member.summary: Defaults which is the value used by Microsoft.Identity.Web.UI.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.ErrorPath
member.summary: Defaults to the value used by Microsoft.Identity.Web.UI.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.ReturnUrl
member.summary: Used to convey the original location of the user before the remote challenge was triggered up to the access denied page.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Constants.ClientAssertion
member.summary: Key for client assertion in token acquisition options.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.DefaultAuthorizationHeaderProvider.UpdateOriginalTokenAcquisitionOptions(Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Since AcquireTokenOptions is recreated, we need to update the original TokenAcquisitionOptions wth the parameters that were updated in the new TokenAcquisitionOptions.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpRequestMessageAuthenticationExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for to configure per-request authentication options when using .
member.remarks: These extension methods enable flexible per-request authentication configuration that can override or supplement the default options configured in the message handler. The methods support both modern .NET (using HttpRequestMessage.Options) and legacy frameworks (using HttpRequestMessage.Properties).

example.para: Setting authentication options with an object:
Configuring authentication options with a delegate:

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpRequestMessageAuthenticationExtensions.WithAuthenticationOptions(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions)
member.summary: Sets authentication options for the HTTP request.
member.param: The HTTP request message to configure.
The authentication options to apply to this request.
member.returns: The same request message for method chaining.
member.remarks: This method will override any existing authentication options set on the request. The options object can be further configured with extension methods from other Microsoft Identity Web packages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpRequestMessageAuthenticationExtensions.WithAuthenticationOptions(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Action{Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions})
member.summary: Configures authentication options for the HTTP request using a delegate.
member.param: The HTTP request message to configure.
A delegate that configures the authentication options.
member.returns: The same request message for method chaining.

remarks.para: If the request already has authentication options configured, the delegate will receive the existing options object to modify. Otherwise, a new instance will be created and passed to the delegate.
This method is particularly useful when you need to apply extension methods from other Microsoft Identity Web packages, such as agent identity methods.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.HttpRequestMessageAuthenticationExtensions.GetAuthenticationOptions(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage)
member.summary: Gets the authentication options that have been set for the HTTP request.
member.param: The HTTP request message to examine.
member.returns:
The if previously set using
or , otherwise .
member.remarks: This method is primarily used internally by but can also be useful for debugging or conditional logic based on authentication configuration.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.IAuthenticationSchemeInformationProvider
member.summary: Provides information about the effective authentication scheme. If passing null or string.Empty, this returns the default authentication scheme.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CerticateObserverAction
member.summary: Action of the token acquirer on the certificate.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CerticateObserverAction.Selected
member.summary: The certificate was selected as a client certificate.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CerticateObserverAction.Deselected
member.summary: The certificate was deselected as a client certificate. This happens when the STS does not accept the certificate any longer.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CerticateObserverAction.SuccessfullyUsed
member.summary: The certificate was successfully used.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg
member.summary: Event argument about the certificate consumption by the app

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg.Action
member.summary: Action on the certificate

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg.Certificate
member.summary: Certificate

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg.CredentialDescription
member.summary: Credential description

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg.ThrownException
member.summary: Gets the exception thrown during the certificate selection or deselection.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.ICertificatesObserver
member.summary: Interface that apps can implement to be notified when a certificate is selected or removed.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.ICertificatesObserver.OnClientCertificateChanged(Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CertificateChangeEventArg)
member.summary: Called when a certificate is selected or removed.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.IDWebErrorMessage
member.summary: Constants related to the error messages.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TestOnly.IManagedIdentityTestHttpClientFactory
member.summary: **TEST-ONLY.** Allows unit tests to supply a custom .

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TestOnly.TokenAcquirerFactoryTesting
member.summary: Class that should only be used in tests, not product code, used to reset the default instance of the token acquirer factory.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TestOnly.TokenAcquirerFactoryTesting.ResetTokenAcquirerFactoryInTest
member.summary: Resets the default instance of the token acquirer factory. Use in tests, but not in production code.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition
member.summary: Interface for the token acquisition service (encapsulating MSAL.NET).

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from an ASP.NET Core web app or web API controller. This method gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user account for which the claims are provided in the member of the controller's parameter.
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Enables to override the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Azure AD B2C UserFlow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest in.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token to call on behalf of the user, the downstream API characterized by its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from an ASP.NET Core web app or web API controller. This method gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user account for which the claims are provided in the current user
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
Enables to override the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Azure AD B2C UserFlow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest in.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token to call on behalf of the user, the downstream API characterized by its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from an ASP.NET Core web app or web API controller. This method gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user account for which the claims are provided in the current user
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Enables to override the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Azure AD B2C UserFlow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest in.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An to call on behalf of the user, the downstream API characterized by its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from an ASP.NET Core web app or web API controller. This method gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user account for which the claims are provided in the current user
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web APIs.
Enables to override the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Azure AD B2C UserFlow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest in.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An to call on behalf of the user, the downstream API characterized by its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires a token from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using the client credentials flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For this flow (client credentials), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires a token from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using the client credentials flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For this flow (client credentials), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires an authentication result from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using the client credentials flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For this flow (client credentials), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, and cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful for multi tenant apps or daemons.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An authentication result for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires an authentication result from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using the client credentials flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For this flow (client credentials), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, and cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful for multi tenant apps or daemons.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An authentication result for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.ReplyForbiddenWithWwwAuthenticateHeader(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalUiRequiredException,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpResponse)
member.summary: Used in web APIs (which therefore cannot have an interaction with the user). Replies to the client through the HttpResponse by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating wwwAuthenticateHeaders so that the client can trigger an interaction with the user so the user can consent to more scopes.
member.param: Scopes to consent to.
triggering the challenge.
The to update.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.ReplyForbiddenWithWwwAuthenticateHeader(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalUiRequiredException,System.String,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpResponse)
member.summary: Used in web APIs (which therefore cannot have an interaction with the user). Replies to the client through the HttpResponse by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating wwwAuthenticateHeaders so that the client can trigger an interaction with the user so the user can consent to more scopes.
member.param: Scopes to consent to.
triggering the challenge.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
The to update.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.GetEffectiveAuthenticationScheme(System.String)
member.summary: Get the effective authentication scheme based on the context.
member.param: Proposed authentication scheme.
member.returns: Effective authenticationScheme which is the authentication scheme if it's not null, or otherwise OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisition.ReplyForbiddenWithWwwAuthenticateHeaderAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalUiRequiredException,Microsoft.AspNetCore.Http.HttpResponse)
member.summary: Used in web APIs (which therefore cannot have an interaction with the user). Replies to the client through the HttpResponse by sending a 403 (forbidden) and populating wwwAuthenticateHeaders so that the client can trigger an interaction with the user so the user can consent to more scopes.
member.param: Scopes to consent to.
triggering the challenge.
The to update.
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisitionInternal
member.summary: Interface for the internal operations of token acquisition service (encapsulating MSAL.NET).

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisitionInternal.AddAccountToCacheFromAuthorizationCodeAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Authentication.OpenIdConnect.AuthorizationCodeReceivedContext,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String)
member.summary: In a web app, adds, to the MSAL.NET cache, the account of the user authenticating to the web app, when the authorization code is received (after the user signed-in and consented) An On-behalf-of token contained in the is added to the cache, so that it can then be used to acquire another token on-behalf-of the same user in order to call to downstream APIs.
member.param: The context used when an 'AuthorizationCode' is received over the OpenIdConnect protocol.
Scopes to request.
Authentication scheme to use.
member.returns: A that represents a completed add to cache operation.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ITokenAcquisitionInternal.RemoveAccountAsync(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,System.String)
member.summary: Removes the account associated with context.HttpContext.User from the MSAL.NET cache.
member.param: Signed in user
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web APIs.
member.returns: A that represents a completed remove from cache operation.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.LoggingEventId
member.summary: EventIds for Logging.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.LoggingOptions
member.summary: LoggingOptions class for passing in Identity specific logging options.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.LoggingOptions.EnablePiiLogging
member.summary: Enable Pii Logging from configuration. Default is false.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.LogMessages
member.summary: Constants related to the log messages.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions
member.summary: Options for configuring authentication using Azure Active Directory. It has both AAD and B2C configuration attributes. Merges the MicrosoftIdentityWebOptions and the ConfidentialClientApplicationOptions.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions.Logger
member.summary: Logger instance for diagnostics.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions.PreparedInstance
member.summary: Id Web will modify the instance so that it can be used by MSAL. This modifies this property so that the original value is not changed.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptionsLogging
member.summary: High-performance logging for MergedOptions operations.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptionsLogging.AuthorityIgnored(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Logs a warning when Authority is configured alongside Instance and/or TenantId.
member.param: The logger instance.
The Authority value that is being ignored.
The Instance value that takes precedence.
The TenantId value that takes precedence.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAppCallsWebApiAuthenticationBuilder
member.summary: Authentication builder returned by the EnableTokenAcquisitionToCallDownstreamApi methods enabling you to decide token cache implementations.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAppCallsWebApiAuthenticationBuilder.AddInMemoryTokenCaches(System.Action{Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenCacheProviders.InMemory.MsalMemoryTokenCacheOptions},System.Action{Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Memory.MemoryCacheOptions})
member.summary: Add in memory token caches, if no other implementation of IMsalTokenCacheProvider was already added.
member.param: to configure.
to configure.
member.returns: the service collection.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAppCallsWebApiAuthenticationBuilder.AddDistributedTokenCaches
member.summary: Add distributed token caches.
member.returns: the service collection.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAuthenticationException
member.summary: Exception thrown when authentication fails during HTTP message handling by .

remarks.para: This exception is typically thrown in the following scenarios:

item.description: No authentication options are configured (neither default nor per-request)
No scopes are specified in the authentication options
Token acquisition fails due to authentication provider issues

remarks.para: Note on WWW-Authenticate Challenge Handling: When a downstream API returns a 401 Unauthorized response with a WWW-Authenticate header containing additional claims (e.g., for Conditional Access), the handler automatically extracts these claims using and attempts to acquire a new token with the requested claims. If this automatic retry succeeds, no exception is thrown. If the retry also fails with a 401, the response is returned to the caller without throwing an exception - the caller should check the status code. Exceptions are only thrown for token acquisition failures, not for HTTP 401 responses themselves.

When handling this exception, examine the property for specific details about what caused the authentication failure. If an inner exception is present, it may contain additional information from the underlying authentication provider.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAuthenticationException.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified error message.
member.param: The error message that explains the reason for the exception.

example.code: throw new MicrosoftIdentityAuthenticationException( "Authentication options must be configured either in default options or per-request using WithAuthenticationOptions().");

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityAuthenticationException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception.
member.param: The error message that explains the reason for the exception.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or if no inner exception is specified.
member.remarks: Use this constructor when you want to preserve the original exception that caused the authentication failure, such as exceptions from the underlying token acquisition provider.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityBaseAuthenticationBuilder
member.summary: Base class for web app and web API Microsoft Identity authentication builders.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityBaseAuthenticationBuilder.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfigurationSection)
member.summary: Constructor.
member.param: The services being configured.
Optional configuration section.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityBaseAuthenticationBuilder.Services
member.summary: The services being configured.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityBaseAuthenticationBuilder.ConfigurationSection
member.summary: Configuration section from which to bind options.
member.remarks: It can be null if the configuration happens with delegates rather than configuration.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler
member.summary: A implementation that automatically adds authorization headers to outgoing HTTP requests using and .

remarks.para: This message handler provides a flexible, composable way to add Microsoft Identity authentication to HttpClient-based code. It serves as an alternative to IDownstreamApi for scenarios where developers want to maintain direct control over HTTP request handling while still benefiting from Microsoft Identity Web's authentication capabilities.

item.description: Automatic authorization header injection for all outgoing requests
Per-request authentication options using extension methods
Automatic WWW-Authenticate challenge handling with token refresh
Support for agent identity and managed identity scenarios

remarks.para: When a downstream API returns a 401 Unauthorized response with a WWW-Authenticate header containing Bearer challenges with additional claims, this handler will automatically attempt to acquire a new token with the requested claims and retry the request. This is particularly useful for Conditional Access scenarios where additional claims are required.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler.#ctor(Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.IAuthorizationHeaderProvider,Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The used to acquire authorization headers for outgoing requests. This is typically obtained from the dependency injection container.
Default authentication options that will be used for all requests unless overridden per-request using . If , each request must specify its own authentication options or an exception will be thrown.
Optional logger for debugging and monitoring authentication operations. If provided, the handler will log information about token acquisition, challenges, and errors.

example.para: Usage without default options (per-request configuration required):
example.code: var logger = serviceProvider.GetService<ILogger<MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler>>(); var handler = new MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler(headerProvider, defaultOptions, logger);

remarks.para: The parameter provides a convenient way to set authentication options that apply to all requests made through this handler instance. Individual requests can still override these defaults using the extension methods.
When is provided, the handler will log at various levels:

item.description: Information: WWW-Authenticate challenge detection and handling

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler.SendAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends an HTTP request with automatic authentication header injection. Handles WWW-Authenticate challenges by attempting token refresh with additional claims if needed.
member.param: The HTTP request message to send.
A cancellation token to cancel operation.
member.returns: The HTTP response message.
member.exception: Thrown when authentication fails, including scenarios where: - No authentication options are configured - No scopes are specified in the options - Token acquisition fails - WWW-Authenticate challenge handling fails

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler.SendWithAuthenticationAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage,Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions,System.Collections.Generic.IList{System.String},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Sends an HTTP request with authentication header injection.
member.param: The HTTP request message.
The authentication options to use.
The scopes for token acquisition.
A cancellation token to cancel operation.
member.returns: The HTTP response message.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler.CreateOptionsWithChallengeClaims(Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new options instance with challenge claims added.
member.param: The original authentication options.
The claims from the WWW-Authenticate challenge.
member.returns: A new options instance with challenge claims configured.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandler.CloneHttpRequestMessageAsync(System.Net.Http.HttpRequestMessage)
member.summary: Clones an HttpRequestMessage for retry scenarios.
member.param: The original request to clone.
member.returns: A cloned HttpRequestMessage.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for authentication. Inherits from to enable compatibility with existing extension methods such as WithAgentIdentity() and WithUserAgentIdentity().

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityMessageHandlerOptions.Scopes
member.summary: Gets or sets the scopes to request for the token.
member.value: A list of scopes required to access the target API. For instance, "user.read mail.read" for Microsoft Graph user permissions. For Microsoft Identity, in the case of application tokens (requested by the app on behalf of itself), there should be only one scope, and it should end with ".default" (e.g., "https://graph.microsoft.com/.default").
member.remarks: This property must contain at least one scope, or the will throw a when processing requests.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions
member.summary: Options for configuring authentication using Azure Active Directory. It has both AAD and B2C configuration attributes.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.Instance
member.summary: Gets or sets the Azure Active Directory instance, e.g. "https://login.microsoftonline.com".

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.TenantId
member.summary: Gets or sets the tenant ID.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.Domain
member.summary: Gets or sets the domain of the Azure Active Directory tenant, e.g. contoso.onmicrosoft.com.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.EditProfilePolicyId
member.summary: Gets or sets the edit profile user flow name for B2C, e.g. b2c_1_edit_profile.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.SignUpSignInPolicyId
member.summary: Gets or sets the sign up or sign in user flow name for B2C, e.g. b2c_1_susi.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ResetPasswordPolicyId
member.summary: Gets or sets the reset password user flow name for B2C, e.g. B2C_1_password_reset.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.DefaultUserFlow
member.summary: Gets the default user flow (which is signUpSignIn).

members.member:
Enables legacy ADAL cache serialization and deserialization. Performance improvements when working with MSAL only apps. Set to true if you have a shared cache with ADAL apps.
The default is false.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.IsB2C
member.summary: Is considered B2C if the attribute SignUpSignInPolicyId is defined.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.HasClientCredentials
member.summary: Is considered to have client credentials if the attribute ClientCertificates or ClientSecret is defined.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ClientCertificates
member.summary: Description of the certificates used to prove the identity of the web app or web API.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.TokenDecryptionCertificates
member.summary: Description of the certificates used to decrypt an encrypted token in a web API.

members.member:
Specifies if the x5c claim (public key of the certificate) should be sent to the STS. Sending the x5c enables application developers to achieve easy certificate rollover in Azure AD: this method will send the public certificate to Azure AD along with the token request, so that Azure AD can use it to validate the subject name based on a trusted issuer policy. This saves the application admin from the need to explicitly manage the certificate rollover (either via portal or PowerShell/CLI operation). For details see https://aka.ms/msal-net-sni.
The default is false.

Requests an auth code for the frontend (SPA using MSAL.js for instance). See https://aka.ms/msal-net/spa-auth-code for details.
The default is false.

Daemon applications can validate a token based on roles, or using the ACL-based authorization pattern to control tokens without a roles claim. If using ACL-based authorization, Microsoft Identity Web will not throw if roles or scopes are not in the Claims. For details see https://aka.ms/ms-identity-web/daemon-ACL.
The default is false.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.UserAssignedManagedIdentityClientId
member.summary: Used, when deployed to Azure, to specify explicitly a user assigned managed identity. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/managed-identities-azure-resources/how-to-manage-ua-identity-portal.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ClientCredentialsUsingManagedIdentity
member.summary: Options for configuring certificateless

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ResetPasswordPath
member.summary: Sets the ResetPassword route path. Defaults to /MicrosoftIdentity/Account/ResetPassword, which is the value used by Microsoft.Identity.Web.UI.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ErrorPath
member.summary: Sets the Error route path. Defaults to the value /MicrosoftIdentity/Account/Error, which is the value used by Microsoft.Identity.Web.UI.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityOptions.ExtraQueryParameters
member.summary: Sets query parameters for the query string in the HTTP request to the IdP.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException
member.summary: Microsoft Identity Web specific exception class for use in Blazor or Razor pages to process the user challenge. Handles the .

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException.MsalUiRequiredException
member.summary: Exception thrown by MSAL when a user challenge is encountered.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException.Scopes
member.summary: Scopes to request.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException.Userflow
member.summary: Specified userflow.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.MicrosoftIdentityWebChallengeUserException.#ctor(Microsoft.Identity.Client.MsalUiRequiredException,System.String[],System.String)
member.summary: Handles the user challenge for Blazor or Razor pages.
member.param: Exception thrown by MSAL when a user challenge is encountered.
Scopes to request.
Userflow used in B2C.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.PrincipalExtensionsForSecurityTokens
member.summary: Extensions to retrieve a from .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.PrincipalExtensionsForSecurityTokens.GetBootstrapToken(System.Security.Principal.IPrincipal)
member.summary: Get the used to call a protected web API.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ServiceCollectionExtensions
member.summary: Extensions for IServiceCollection for startup initialization of web APIs.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddTokenAcquisition(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Add the token acquisition service.
member.param: Service collection.
Specifies if an instance of should be a singleton.
member.returns: The service collection.
member.example:
This method is typically called from the ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) in Startup.cs. Note that the implementation of the token cache can be chosen separately.
// Token acquisition service and its cache implementation as a session cache services.AddTokenAcquisition() .AddDistributedMemoryCache() .AddSession() .AddSessionBasedTokenCache();

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerExtensions
member.summary: Extension methods for TokenAcquirer

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerExtensions.GetFicTokenAsync(Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.ITokenAcquirer,Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions,System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Used to exchange a client assertion for a token.
member.param: Token acquirer from which to get a Federation Identity Credential token. A FIC token is used as a client assertion to get another token.
Options for the token acquisition (for instance to override the tenant programmatically).
Client assertion to use for the token acquisition. If null, the FIC Token will be acquired using the credentials associated with token token acquirer (that is the named from the )

Scope for the token acquisition. By default this is api://AzureAdTokenExchange/.default. for other clouds, you would need to override it:
api://AzureAdTokenExchange/.defaultfor public cloud api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina/.defaultfor China cloud api://AzureADTokenExchangeFrance/.defaultfor Bleu api://AzureADTokenExchangeGermany/.defaultfor the German cloud
Cancellation token.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerExtensions.WithClientAssertion(Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions,System.String)
member.summary: Consider the client assertion for the token request.
member.param: Options (can be null)
client assertion (shouldn't be null)
member.returns: The modified options. New options with the signed assertion if was null.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory
member.summary: Factory of a token acquirer.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.Configuration
member.summary: Configuration. By default the configuration contains the content of the appsettings.json file, provided this file is copied in the folder of the app.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.ServiceProvider
member.summary: Service Provider. The service provider is only available once the factory was built using .

members.member:
Services. Used in the initialization phase
will be thrown if you try to access Services after you called .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.#ctor
member.summary: Constructor

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.GetDefaultInstance(System.String)
member.summary: Get the default instance. Use this method to retrieve the instance, optionally add some services to the service collection, and build the instance.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.Build
member.summary: Build the Token acquirer. This does the composition of all the services you have added to , and returns a service provider, which you can then use to get access to the services you have added.
member.example:
The following example shows how you add Microsoft GraphServiceClient to the services and use it after you've built the token acquirer factory. The authentication is handled transparently based on the information in the appsettings.json.
[!code-csharp[ConvertType](~/../tests/DevApps/daemon-app/daemon-console-calling-msgraph/Program.cs?highlight=7)]

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.PreBuild
member.summary: Additional processing before the Build (adds an in-memory token cache if no cache was added)

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.defaultInstance
member.summary: Default instance

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.ResetDefaultInstance
member.summary: Resets the default instance. Useful for tests as token acquirer factory is a singleton in most configurations (except ASP.NET Core)

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquirerFactory.DefineConfiguration(Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfigurationBuilder)
member.summary: Adding additional configuration and returns the base path for configuration files
member.returns: Returns the base path for configuration files

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition
member.summary: Token acquisition service.
LoggingMessage class for TokenAcquisition.
Portion of the TokenAcquisition class that handles logic unique to managed identity.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition._applicationsByAuthorityClientId
member.summary: Important: call GetOrBuildConfidentialClientApplication instead of accessing _applicationsByAuthorityClientId directly. Write access to this dictionary is synchronized.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition._scopesRequestedByMsal
member.summary: Scopes which are already requested by MSAL.NET. They should not be re-requested;.

member.name: F:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition._metaTenantIdentifiers
member.summary: Meta-tenant identifiers which are not allowed in client credentials.

Constructor of the TokenAcquisition service. This requires the Azure AD Options to configure the confidential client application and a token cache provider. This constructor is called by ASP.NET Core dependency injection.
member.param: The App token cache provider.
Host of the token acquisition.
HTTP client factory.
Logger.
Service provider.
Credential loader used to provide the credentials.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetApplicationKey(Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions)
member.summary: Allows creation of confidential client applications targeting regional and global authorities when supporting managed identities.
member.param: Merged configuration options
member.returns: Concatenated string of authority, cliend id and azure region

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from a web app or web API controller, this method retrieves an access token for a downstream API using; 1) the token cache (for web apps and web APIs) if a token exists in the cache 2) or the on-behalf-of flow in web APIs, for the user account that is ascertained from claims provided in the current claims principal. instance of the current HttpContext.
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web APIs.

Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest.
Azure AD B2C user flow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant, like where the user is a guest.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition options object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token to call the downstream API and populated with this downstream API's scopes.
member.remarks: Calling this method from a web API supposes that you have previously called, in a method called by JwtBearerOptions.Events.OnTokenValidated, the HttpContextExtensions.StoreTokenUsedToCallWebAPI method passing the validated token (as a JwtSecurityToken or JSonWebToken). Calling it from a web app supposes that you have previously called AddAccountToCacheFromAuthorizationCodeAsync from a method called by OpenIdConnectOptions.Events.OnAuthorizationCodeReceived.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.ResolveTenant(System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions)
member.summary: Resolves the tenant based on if the tenant is already set or the TenantId configured in the options or the AppHomeTenantId if the TenantId is a meta tenant.
member.param: Provided tenant or null if not provided
Merged configuration from which to retrieve tenant value as necessary
member.returns: Resolved tenant

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetAuthenticationResultForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires an authentication result from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using either a client credentials or managed identity flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials for client credentials or https://aka.ms/Entra/ManagedIdentityOverview for managed identity.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For these flows (client credentials or managed identity), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, and cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
AuthenticationScheme to use.
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful for multi tenant apps or daemons.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An authentication result for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForAppAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Acquires a token from the authority configured in the app, for the confidential client itself (not on behalf of a user) using the client credentials flow. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-client-credentials.
member.param: The scope requested to access a protected API. For this flow (client credentials), the scope should be of the form "{ResourceIdUri/.default}" for instance https://management.azure.net/.default or, for Microsoft Graph, https://graph.microsoft.com/.default as the requested scopes are defined statically with the application registration in the portal, and cannot be overridden in the application, as you can request a token for only one resource at a time (use several calls to get tokens for other resources).
AuthenticationScheme to use.
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful for multi tenant apps or daemons.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token for the app itself, based on its scopes.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetAccessTokenForUserAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions)
member.summary: Typically used from a web app or web API controller, this method retrieves an access token for a downstream API using; 1) the token cache (for web apps and web APIs) if a token exists in the cache 2) or the on-behalf-of flow in web APIs, for the user account that is ascertained from the claims provided in the current claims principal. instance of the current HttpContext.
member.param: Scopes to request for the downstream API to call.
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
Enables overriding of the tenant/account for the same identity. This is useful in the cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Azure AD B2C user flow to target.

Optional claims principal representing the user. If not provided, will use the signed-in user (in a web app), or the user for which the token was received (in a web API) cases where a given account is a guest in other tenants, and you want to acquire tokens for a specific tenant.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.
member.returns: An access token to call the downstream API and populated with this downstream API's scopes.
member.remarks: Calling this method from a web API supposes that you have previously called, in a method called by JwtBearerOptions.Events.OnTokenValidated, the HttpContextExtensions.StoreTokenUsedToCallWebAPI method passing the validated token (as a JwtSecurityToken or JSonWebToken). Calling it from a web app supposes that you have previously called AddAccountToCacheFromAuthorizationCodeAsync from a method called by OpenIdConnectOptions.Events.OnAuthorizationCodeReceived.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.RemoveAccountAsync(System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal,System.String)
member.summary: Removes the account associated with context.HttpContext.User from the MSAL.NET cache.
member.param: User
Authentication scheme. If null, will use OpenIdConnectDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web app, and JwtBearerDefault.AuthenticationScheme if called from a web API.
member.returns: A that represents a completed account removal operation.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.BuildConfidentialClientApplicationAsync(Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions)
member.summary: Creates an MSAL confidential client application.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.NotifyCertificateSelection(Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions,Microsoft.Identity.Client.IConfidentialClientApplication,Microsoft.Identity.Web.Experimental.CerticateObserverAction,System.Exception)
member.summary: Find the certificate used by the app and fire the event to notify the client app that a certificate was selected/unselected.
member.param: The thrown exception, if any.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.CheckAssertionsForInjectionAttempt(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Checks assertion and sub_assertion passed from merging extra query parameters to ensure they do not contain unsupported characters.
member.param: The assertion.
The sub_assertion.

member.summary: Gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user described by its claimsPrincipal.
member.param: Claims principal for the user on behalf of whom to get a token.
Scopes for the downstream API to call.
(optional) TenantID based on a specific tenant for which to acquire a token to access the scopes on behalf of the user described in the claimsPrincipal.
Merged options.
Azure AD B2C user flow to target.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.

member.summary: Gets an access token for a downstream API on behalf of the user whose account is passed as an argument.
member.param: User IAccount for which to acquire a token. See .
Scopes for the downstream API to call.
TenantID based on a specific tenant for which to acquire a token to access the scopes on behalf of the user.
Merged options.
Azure AD B2C user flow.
Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.OverrideClientAssertionIfNeeded``1(Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions,Microsoft.Identity.Client.AbstractConfidentialClientAcquireTokenParameterBuilder{``0})
member.summary: Temporary. Replace with Builder.WithClientAssertion when MSAL.NET supports it.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.Logger.TokenAcquisitionError(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Logger for handling MSAL exceptions in TokenAcquisition.
member.param: ILogger.
Specific log message from TokenAcquisition.
Exception from MSAL.NET.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.Logger.TokenAcquisitionMsalAuthenticationResultTime(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.Int64,System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Logger for handling information specific to MSAL in token acquisition.
member.param: ILogger.
durationTotalInMs.
durationInHttpInMs.
durationInCacheInMs.
cache or IDP.
correlationId.
cacheRefreshReason.
Exception from MSAL.NET.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetOrBuildManagedIdentityApplicationAsync(Microsoft.Identity.Web.MergedOptions,Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.ManagedIdentityOptions)
member.summary: Gets a cached ManagedIdentityApplication object or builds a new one if not found.
member.param: The configuration options for the app.
The configuration specific to managed identity.
member.returns: The application object used to request a token with managed identity.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.BuildManagedIdentityApplication(Microsoft.Identity.Client.AppConfig.ManagedIdentityId,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.String},System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a managed identity client application.
member.param: Indicates if system-assigned or user-assigned managed identity is used.
Indicates the capabilities of the managed identity application.
Indicates if logging that may contain personally identifiable information is enabled.
member.returns: A managed identity application.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisition.GetCacheKeyForManagedId(Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.ManagedIdentityOptions)
member.summary: Gets the key value for the Managed Identity cache, the default key for system-assigned identity is used if there is no clientId for a user-assigned identity specified. The method is internal rather than private for testing purposes.
member.param: Holds the clientId for managed identity if none is present.
member.returns: A key value for the Managed Identity cache.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions
member.summary: Options for TokenAcquisition add-ins. These options consist in a set of events, that can be subscribed to by add-ins or parts of the add-ins.

member.name: E:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions.OnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForApp
member.summary: Event fired when a client credential flow request is being built.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions.InvokeOnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForApp(Microsoft.Identity.Client.AcquireTokenForClientParameterBuilder,Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions)
member.summary: Invoke the OnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForApp event.

member.name: E:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions.OnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUser
member.summary: Event fired when a ROPC flow request is being built.

member.name: E:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions.OnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUserAsync
member.summary: Event fired when a ROPC flow request is being built.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionExtensionOptions.InvokeOnBeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUserAsync(Microsoft.Identity.Client.AcquireTokenByUsernameAndPasswordConfidentialParameterBuilder,Microsoft.Identity.Abstractions.AcquireTokenOptions,System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal)
member.summary: Invoke the BeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUser event.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.BeforeTokenAcquisitionForApp
member.summary: Signature for token acquisition extensions that act on the request builder, for an app token
member.param: Builder
Token acquisition options for the request. Can be null.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.BeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUser
member.summary: Signature for token acquisition extensions that act on the request builder, for ROPC flow.
member.param: Builder
Token acquisition options for the request. Can be null.
User claims.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.BeforeTokenAcquisitionForTestUserAsync
member.summary: Signature for token acquisition extensions that act on the request builder, for ROPC flow (Async version).
member.param: Builder
Token acquisition options for the request. Can be null.
User claims.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions
member.summary: Options passed-in to create the token acquisition object which calls into MSAL .NET.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions.PoPConfiguration
member.summary: Modifies the token acquisition request so that the acquired token is a Proof of Possession token (PoP), rather than a Bearer token. PoP tokens are similar to Bearer tokens, but are bound to the HTTP request and to a cryptographic key, which MSAL can manage. See https://aka.ms/msal-net-pop.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions.CancellationToken
member.summary: Cancellation token to be used when calling the token acquisition methods.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.TokenAcquisitionOptions.Clone
member.summary: Clone the options (to be able to override them).
member.returns: A clone of the options.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Internal.WebApiBuilders
member.summary: Web API authentication builder.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Identity.Web.Internal.WebApiBuilders.EnableTokenAcquisition(System.Action{Microsoft.Identity.Client.ConfidentialClientApplicationOptions},System.String,Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration.IConfigurationSection)
member.summary: Allows a higher level abstraction of security token (i.e. System.IdentityModel.Tokens.Jwt and more modern, Microsoft.IdentityModel.JsonWebTokens) to be used with Microsoft Identity Web. Developers should continue to use `EnableTokenAcquisitionToCallDownstreamApi`. This API is not considered part of the public API and may change.
member.param: The action to configure .
Authentication scheme.
The services being configured.
IConfigurationSection.
member.returns: The authentication builder to chain.
